perturbation.original_text: Unless otherwise provided for in this agreement, the above- mentioned obligations of this Section II shall apply in each case for a period of 3 (three) years after the point in time at which the respective confidential information was disclosed by M&R or made accessible to the Partner.
perturbation.changed_text: Unless otherwise provided for in this agreement, the above- mentioned obligations of this Section II shall apply in each case for a period of 1 (one) year after the point in time at which the respective confidential information was disclosed by M&R or made accessible to the Partner.
perturbation.explanation: The original text sets the confidentiality period for 3 years. The changed text shortens it to 1 year. This creates a direct contradiction within Section II regarding the duration of the non-disclosure obligations. This impacts Section VII where it states: 'This agreement shall become effective upon signing and has a term of 5 (five) years.', making the term of the agreement, longer than some obligations, creating a conflict.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This agreement shall become effective upon signing and has a term of 5 (five) years. Without prejudice to the provision in Section II, last paragraph, the non -disclosure obligation for business secrets and know -how shall, however, continue to appl y after termination of this agreement unless one of the exceptions described in Section III , Subsection c), d) or e) has occurred, for which the burden of proof lies with the Partner. Upon termination of this agreement, all the confidential information made available by M&R shall be returned to M&R on request of M&R, all copies that were made shall be destroyed or handed over to M&R and all stored information shall be deleted. Confident ial information that has to be kept in order to comply with legal obligations, in particular archiving obligations, shall be excepted from that.
perturbation.justification: The changed text reduces the confidentiality period from 3 years to 1 year. This directly contradicts the section that states the agreement has a term of 5 years. The explanation specifies this conflict, making it a clear inconsistency regarding the duration of obligations compared to the overall agreement term, which is a strong in-text contradiction as defined by 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Excepted from the non- disclosure obligation above shall be know -how and information a) which was apparent to or generally known by the Partner or was state of the art already; b) which was already known by the Partner at the time of disclosure; c) which subsequent ly becomes apparent or generally known or state of the art through no fault of the Partner; d) which is disclosed or made accessible to the Partner by a third party who has the right to do so; e) regarding which M&R previously agreed to a passing on, disclosure or making accessible to third parties in writing.
perturbation.changed_text: There are no exceptions to the non-disclosure obligation. All know-how and information, regardless of its source or prior knowledge, is subject to the confidentiality requirements outlined in this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text lists several exceptions to the non-disclosure obligation. The changed text removes all exceptions, stating that *all* information is confidential. This directly contradicts the original conditions under which information would be considered exempt. This can create a conflict if a third party has the right to share the information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: There are no exceptions to the non-disclosure obligation. All know-how and information, regardless of its source or prior knowledge, is subject to the confidentiality requirements outlined in this agreement. The burden of proof for the presence of an exception in the above- mentioned sense lies with the Partner. If the Partner or his respective general managers, employees, representatives or consultants are requested or obliged to disclose confidential information by a court, M&R shall be notified of that so timely as to enable M&R to initiate immediate legal acti on in court or to use another suitable legal remedy. If this immediate legal action and/or a different legal remedy is not obtained within a reasonable period of time, the Partner that is obliged to disclose shall only disclose the part of the confidential information to the disclosure of which he is obliged by law.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states 'There are no exceptions to the non-disclosure obligation' while the contradicted text discusses exceptions and procedures for disclosing confidential information under legal obligation. This creates a direct contradiction. The original text allowed for exceptions, while the changed text removes them entirely. Therefore, the changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text, as the two cannot be logically reconciled. The explanation provided also states that this change causes a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Insofar as the Partner employs subcontractors for the fulfillment of his duties from the scope of the project, the Partner shall be obliged to bind them to secrecy as well in wri ting in a scope according to this non -disclosure agreement. Proof of this obligation has to be shown to M&R upon request.
perturbation.changed_text: The Partner is not required to impose any secrecy obligations on subcontractors. Any confidentiality agreements with subcontractors are at the Partner's sole discretion.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates the Partner to bind subcontractors to secrecy. The changed text states the Partner is *not* required to do so. This creates a contradiction regarding the responsibilities of the Partner regarding subcontractors' confidentiality obligations. This creates a conflict in section VI, since they can be held liable if the breach is done through subcontractors.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event of a culpable breach, the Partner shall be liable to M&R for compensation for the damage caused and shall keep M&R indemnified from all resulting claims and costs. If it is certain that confidential information found its way to third parties from the sphere of the Partner, a culpable (at least negligent) breach of non- disclosure obligations shall be assumed - except when there is evidence to t he contrary. 1FO-3-01-19_Geheimhaltungsvereinbarung_en Rev. 01 Page 3 of 4 issued: H. Bauer -Kieslinger/18.05. 2016 checked: R. Stokan/19.05. 2016 released: W. Schwarz/19.05. 2016 The Partner shall equally be liable for the conduct of his general manager, employees, representatives, consultants, subcontractors, vicarious agents and all other parties that are involved in the project by him.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the Partner is not required to impose secrecy obligations on subcontractors, which directly contradicts the original text mandating such obligations. The contradicted text holds the Partner liable for the conduct of subcontractors. This contradiction creates a conflict because if the Partner is not required to impose secrecy on subcontractors, they may not be liable for a breach by the subcontractor under the contradicted clause, which assumes the Partner is responsible for the actions of their subcontractors.

perturbation.original_text: All rights in the confidential information shall remain with M&R.
perturbation.changed_text: The Partner gains full ownership of the confidential information 12 months after its disclosure, including the right to use, modify, and share it without restriction.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states M&R retains all rights to the confidential information. The changed text grants the Partner ownership after 12 months. This is a direct contradiction concerning who owns and controls the confidential information, and can affect section V where it states: 'No relations extending beyond that between the contracting parties, in particular no powers of agency of the one contracting par ty for the other contracting party, can be derived from this agreement.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: No Granting of Rights The communication, disclosure or making accessible of confidential information grants the Partner no rights, li censes or industrial property rights of any kind whatsoever. The Partner gains full ownership of the confidential information 12 months after its disclosure, including the right to use, modify, and share it without restriction. No relations extending beyond that between the contracting parties, in particular no powers of agency of the one contracting par ty for the other contracting party, can be derived from this agreement. In particular, this agreement does not oblige the contracting parties to disclose confidential information to the respective other party or to enter into contractual relations with the other contracting party in the future.
perturbation.justification: The original text states M&R retains all rights to the confidential information. The changed text grants the Partner ownership after 12 months. This directly contradicts the 'No Granting of Rights' clause in the contradicted text, which states that the communication of confidential information grants the partner no rights, licenses, or industrial property rights of any kind. The changed text introduces a direct contradiction by granting ownership, which is a right, and therefore creates an in-text contradiction as per the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Upon termination of this agreement, all the confidential information made available by M&R shall be returned to M&R on request of M&R, all copies that were made shall be destroyed or handed over to M&R and all stored information shall be deleted. Confident ial information that has to be kept in order to comply with legal obligations, in particular archiving obligations, shall be excepted from that.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon termination of this agreement, the Partner is not obligated to return or destroy any confidential information. The Partner may retain and use the information for any purpose.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates the return/destruction of confidential information upon termination. The changed text states the Partner has no such obligation. This directly contradicts the post-termination obligations concerning confidential information. This will have an impact on Section VIII where it states: 'M&R accepts no liability for the use or the t rust in the correctness or completeness of the confidential information disclosed to the Partner.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: M&R accepts no liability for the use or the t rust in the correctness or completeness of the confidential information disclosed to the Partner.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires the Partner to return or destroy confidential information upon termination, while the changed text explicitly states the Partner has no such obligation and may retain and use the information. This directly contradicts the expectation within the contract regarding post-termination handling of confidential information. The contradicted text, 'M&R accepts no liability for the use or the trust in the correctness or completeness of the confidential information disclosed to the Partner,' is directly impacted because the changed text gives the partner the right to continue using confidential information, while the original text expected the partner to return or destroy the information. The change introduces a direct conflict regarding the liability since the partner can now use the information after termination, making the original liability clause potentially problematic and creates an in-text contradiction as defined by 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction'.
